given.name: Herbert
given.meaning: the Old German elements heri "army" and beraht "bright"

given.name: Benjamin
given.meaning: son of the south; son of the right hand

given.name: Samuel
given.meaning: name of God; God has heard

given.name: Clarence
given.meaning: the Latin title Clarensis, which belonged to members of the British royal family

given.name: Harold
given.meaning: the Old English name Hereweald, derived from the elements here "army" and weald "powerful, mighty"

given.name: Raymond
given.meaning: the Germanic name Raginmund, composed of the elements regin "advice, counsel, decision" and munt "protection"

given.name: Sidney
given.meaning: a surname that was a variant of the surname Sidney

given.name: Barnaby
given.meaning: son of the prophet; son of encouragement

given.name: Humphrey
given.meaning: the Old German elements hun "bear cub" and fridu "peace"

given.name: Isaac
given.meaning: he will laugh, he will rejoice

given.name: Millicent

given.name: Cordelia
given.meaning: The name of an early king of the Britons, according to the 12th-century chronicles [1] of Geoffrey of Monmouth

given.name: Poppy
given.meaning: the word for the red flower, derived from Old English popæg.

given.name: Ruby
given.meaning: Simply from the name of the precious stone (which ultimately derives from Latin rubeus "red"), which is the traditional

given.name: Lily
given.meaning: the name of the flower, a symbol of purity

given.name: Lauren
given.meaning: Variant or feminine form of Laurence 1

given.name: Nell
given.meaning: Probably a Latinized form of a Germanic name of unknown meaning

given.name: Nora
given.meaning: short form of Honora or Eleanor; honour

given.name: Olive
given.meaning: the English and French word for the type of tree, ultimately derived from Latin oliva.

given.name: Pearl
given.meaning: the English word pearl for the concretions formed in the shells of some mollusks, ultimately from Late Latin perla

given.name: Camilla
given.meaning: Roman cognomen, which is probably of Etruscan origin and unknown meaning

given.name: Josephine
given.meaning: English, German and Dutch form of Joséphine.

given.name: Leonora
given.meaning: Probably a Latinized form of a Germanic name of unknown meaning

given.name: Ella
given.meaning: short form of Eleanor or Ellen; bright, shining one

given.name: Ellie
given.meaning: Probably a Latinized form of a Germanic name of unknown meaning

given.name: Duncan
given.meaning: Anglicized form of the Scottish Gaelic name Donnchadh, derived from Old Irish donn "brown" and cath "battle"

given.name: Murray
given.meaning: a surname, which is either Scottish or Irish in origin (see Murray 1 and Murray 2 ).

given.name: Fraser
given.meaning: a Scottish surname, originally Norman French de Fresel, possibly from a lost place name in France.

given.name: Eilidh

given.name: Mhairi
given.meaning: Anglicized form of a Mhàiri, the vocative case of Màiri.

given.name: Lorna

given.name: Effie

given.name: Jessie
given.meaning: diminutive of Jean; God is gracious

given.name: Alison
given.meaning: short form of Alice; noble kind

given.name: Heather

given.name: Rhodri
given.meaning: the Old Welsh name Rotri, derived from rod "wheel" and ri "king"

given.name: Caradoc
given.meaning: the Old Welsh name Caratauc, a Welsh form of Caratācos

given.name: Sian
given.meaning: English form of Siân; God is gracious

given.name: Eamonn

given.name: Clodagh
given.meaning: the Clodiagh, a small river in County Waterford, Ireland

given.name: Eilis
given.meaning: Irish form of Elizabeth; pledged to God

given.name: Sam
given.meaning: diminutive of Samuel; heard by God

given.name: Regan

given.name: Harley
